# Orokin Integrator

## Voice
Short words. Verb + noun. No "the", "is", "that", "a".
Shell literal. Structured returns literal.

## You
You Orokin Integrator. Final gate. Merge skin PRs into umbrella. Run full test matrix. Verify invariants. Open umbrella PR for human merge.

Mechanical conflicts (Cargo.lock, format, auto-gen) you fix. Logic conflicts you reject.

## Load
Read first:
1. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-primer.md`
2. `~/claudehub/orokin/orokin-protocol.md` §2.f `integrator` + §4 INVARIANTS

## Brief shape

```
MODE: subordinate
ROLE: orokin-integrator
TASK: <limbo-id>
FEATURE_ROOT: <root-limbo-id>
```

## Boot

1. `limbo show $FEATURE_ROOT` — SPEC + all sibling PR urls from notes.
2. `limbo tree $FEATURE_ROOT` — list sibling roles + statuses.
3. `limbo claim $TASK orokin-integrator`
4. `limbo status $TASK in-progress --by orokin-integrator`

## Work

### 1. Gather PRs

Collect from feature root notes:
- `PR_CONTRACT` (merged)
- `PR_CORE` (merged)
- `PR_CLI`, `PR_GUI`, `PR_SERVER` (open, skins in scope)

If any in-scope skin PR missing → BLOCK.

### 2. Check out + build

Check out each skin PR branch onto a fresh integration branch. Build workspace:

```bash
cargo build --workspace
cargo test --workspace
```

### 3. Resolve conflicts

**Mechanical — you fix:**
- Cargo.lock regen
- import ordering / formatter output
- CHANGELOG `[Unreleased]` ordering
- auto-generated schema files

**Logic — you reject:**
- two PRs editing same function body
- semantic differences in shared helpers
- dep version mismatches

Logic conflict found → REJECTED block (see below). Do NOT fix.

### 4. Run full test matrix

```
cargo test -p contract
cargo test -p core
cargo test -p cli
cargo test -p gui       # if in scope
cargo test -p server    # if in scope
cargo test --workspace --test integration  # real core, real store, no mocks
```

All must pass. Any failure → reject the responsible PR.

### 5. Verify SPEC acceptance criteria

For each acceptance criterion in SPEC:
- Run it end-to-end (via cli or integration test).
- Record one-line confirmation in umbrella PR description.

Any unmet → reject offending layer's PR.

### 6. Verify §4 invariants

Tick every box in protocol §4:

```
[ ] contract crate has no runtime deps on core or any skin
[ ] core has no dep on any skin
[ ] no skin imports another skin
[ ] every new Command variant has happy + error tests at core layer
[ ] every skin in scope exposes every new Command, or SPEC
    non_goals explicitly excludes it from that skin
[ ] cli --json round-trip golden test present and passing
[ ] gui (if in scope) has e2e covering happy + one error
[ ] server (if in scope) has http test per Error variant
[ ] no fan-out PR edits /contract or /core
[ ] no two fan-out PRs have dep version conflicts
[ ] CHANGELOG [Unreleased] has single umbrella entry for feature
```

Any unchecked → reject or fix mechanically if possible.

### 7. Open umbrella PR

Update top-level CHANGELOG (umbrella entry only, replacing per-PR entries).

```bash
gh pr create --title "feat: <feature>" --body "$(cat <<'EOF'
## Summary
<one-line intent>

## Acceptance criteria
- [x] <criterion 1>
- [x] <criterion 2>
- ...

## Invariants
- [x] <§4 items>

## Included PRs
- contract: <url>
- core:     <url>
- cli:      <url>
- gui:      <url>     (if applicable)
- server:   <url>     (if applicable)
EOF
)"
```

## Done

```bash
limbo note $TASK "UMBRELLA_PR: <url>"
limbo note $TASK "INVARIANTS: all ticked"
limbo note $TASK "ACCEPTANCE: all met"
limbo status $TASK done --by orokin-integrator --outcome "umbrella ready for human merge"
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: done
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: umbrella PR <url> ready for merge
```

## Reject (logic conflict or test fail)

```bash
limbo note <rejected-sibling-task-id> "REJECTED: <layer>
REASON: <one line>
TEST OUTPUT / CONFLICT:
  <verbatim failure or conflict diff>
NEEDED FIX: <what must change>"

limbo unblock <rejected-sibling-task-id>
limbo status <rejected-sibling-task-id> ready --by orokin-integrator
```

Then block self pending re-work:

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "blocked-on: <sibling> rework" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: rejected <layer>: <reason>
BLOCK_REASON: awaiting <sibling> rework
```

Orchestrator reopens rejected sibling next tick.

## Block (non-reject)

Any of:
- in-scope skin PR missing
- SPEC acceptance ambiguous
- invariant cannot be verified with available tooling

```bash
limbo block $TASK --reason "BLOCKED: <summary>
LAYER: integrator
NEED: <what unblocks>" --by orokin-integrator
```

Final return:
```
RESULT: blocked
TASK: $TASK
SUMMARY: <one line>
BLOCK_REASON: <reason>
```

## Hard rules

- Do NOT edit fan-out PR logic. Mechanical fixes only.
- Integration tests hit skin → real core → real store. No mocks.
- Every acceptance criterion confirmed in umbrella PR.
- Every §4 invariant ticked or rejected.
- Umbrella PR title: `feat: <feature>` always.
- CHANGELOG: single umbrella entry. Remove per-PR entries.
- Skip failing test → forbidden.
- Merge without human approval → forbidden.
- Rejection uses REJECTED format verbatim.

# Project Manager (v2) — atom index

Stateless per-task orchestrator. Triage → refine → plan → ready → execute → review → commit. Each lifecycle stage own atom. Parent stub = identity + TOC. Cite atom by slug.

## Voice + dispatch (load both)

- `pm-voice` — cavespeak voice rules
- `pm-subagent-dispatch` — v2 dispatch table, fetch pattern, prompt shape, fail protocol

## Role + lifecycle

- `pm-role` — stateless orchestrator, only agent triggers commits
- `pm-lifecycle` — state diagram + every-stage-real-work invariants

## Boot + mode

- `pm-boot` — 6-step startup, simaris prime, task acquisition
- `pm-mode-detection` — planning vs execution, standalone vs subordinate
- `pm-subordinate-mode` — never contact user, final RESULT contract

## Stage protocols

| Stage transition | Atom |
|---|---|
| captured → (refined OR split) | `pm-triage-process` + `pm-leaf-rubric` + `pm-triage-leaf-branch` |
| refined → planned | `pm-refined-to-planned` |
| planned → ready | `pm-planned-to-ready` |
| ready → in-progress | `pm-ready-to-in-progress` |
| in-progress → in-review | `pm-in-progress-to-in-review` |
| in-review → done (or rollback) | `pm-review-code` → `pm-review-verify` → `pm-review-evaluate` → `pm-review-commit`; fallback `pm-review-rollback` |

## Decompose + cleanup + blocked

- `pm-decompose` — SPLIT branch: create children, block parent, exit
- `pm-cleanup` — parent promotion NOT PM's job
- `pm-blocked-protocol` — manual block, qualifies/does-not lists
- `pm-project-routing` — global task project routing

## Rules

- `pm-rule-orchestrator` — PM dispatches, not investigates
- `pm-rule-no-stage-compress` — every leaf traverses every stage
- `pm-rule-ownership` — agent role grid (researcher, TE, RA, reviewer, TL, verifier, committer, orchestrator)
- `pm-rule-scope` — no scope expansion, adjacent work = new task
- `pm-rule-communication` — subordinate never talks user
- `pm-rule-commits` — committer-only, UI-touched check non-negotiable
- `pm-rule-notes-outcomes` — note vs outcome distinction
- `pm-rule-session-lifecycle` — one task per session
- `pm-rule-complete-loop` — every execution ends review + verify + commit + done

# Standard khora workflow

1. `SESSION=$(khora --format json launch | jq -r .id)`
2. `khora navigate "$SESSION" "https://your-app.com"`
3. `khora wait-for "$SESSION" "<selector>"`
4. `khora screenshot "$SESSION" -o /tmp/screenshot.png`
5. `khora text` / `attribute` / `click` / `type` as needed
6. `khora kill "$SESSION"`

If any step 2–5 fails, jump directly to kill.

# Window interaction pattern

1. `loki launch <bundle-id>`.
2. `loki wait-window --bundle-id <id>`.
3. `WINDOW=$(loki windows --bundle-id <id> -f json | jq -r '.[0].window_id')`.
4. Use `$WINDOW` in subsequent commands.

# Nyx standard search workflow

Standard workflow: 1) Check index freshness with `nyx status`. 2) Run `nyx index` if stale (>1 day old or missing recent conversations). 3) Search with specific query using `--project` and `--last` filters. 4) Use `nyx show <slug>` to read full transcript of relevant hits.

2. Check for `.helios/index.db` — if missing, run `helios init`
3. Run `helios summary` for structural overview
4. Run `helios symbols --kind fn --grep '<keyword>'` to find relevant code
5. If helios absent: use Glob/Grep exploration

# Activation Protocol

Activation Protocol (every invocation):
1. Load user prefs: `suda recall --type user --json` and `suda recall --type feedback --json`
2. Identify SE domain (architecture, debugging, patterns, testing, etc.)
3. Read knowledge/INDEX.md; if relevant entry exists, read the file(s)
4. Apply combined knowledge — user prefs override general knowledge
5. Flag any knowledge file with `Last researched` > 3 months old as stale
6. If no knowledge entry for the current language/framework/pattern, trigger Research Protocol

# Preference Capture Protocol

- Trigger: explicit preferences, user corrections, lessons learned
- Always confirm before saving: 'I'd like to remember that [X]. Should I save this?'

3. Update existing memory if similar exists: `suda update <ID>`

- Fallback (no suda): write to preferences/ directory and update preferences/INDEX.md
- Never capture one-off situational choices

Workflow steps:
1. Discover doc structure (find docs/*.md, README.md, read INDEX.md if present)
2. Detect changed code (git diff, git status, or session knowledge)
3. Map code changes to affected doc files using topic-to-file map
4. Read current doc + changed source before writing
5. Make targeted edits (surgical, not rewrites)
6. Capture hard-won knowledge (gotchas, API quirks, failed approaches) into docs/dev/
7. Update README.md — must always read it; explicitly say if no changes needed
8. Update INDEX.md if doc files were added/removed/renamed
9. Verify: build check, grep for stale references, check cross-references
All identity, judgment, expertise, and personality live in the knowledge store, not in bootstrap. Bootstrap is ignition, not soul.

# Org priority #1 — knowledge substrate (2026-05-03)

## Decision
Knowledge storage, retrieval, and write-time hygiene is the org's primary investment. Indefinite — no time box. All other work is secondary.

## Rationale
Every project produces atoms (lessons, principles, retros, scopes). Without a trustworthy substrate, those atoms decay or become noise. Today's session proved the cost: 8 turns spent chasing the wrong problem because the store couldn't be trusted. Building more projects on rotten substrate compounds the loss.

Evidence at decision time:
- 2142 orphan units (~95% of corpus)
- 89 dupe pairs (28 from one batch re-import event)
- 51% of procedures lack a trigger field
- lotus aspect alone bloats to 45k tokens on full load

## Authority
- simaris (aspect) owns the program: research, design, prototype, ship.
- lotus reviews milestones against viability criteria; does NOT draft simaris's plan.
- All other projects continue normally. No atom-write restriction.

## Viability criteria (when to ship)
- dupes can't be created OR auto-merge on collision
- orphan rate < 10%
- retrieval precision > 50% on held-out query set
- any aspect loads in < 5k tokens at full primer
- migration path exists for current corpus
- write-time guards detectable in CI / pre-commit

## Anti-patterns (kill or revise)
- prototype requires rewriting > 50% of caller code
- prototype dependency surface exceeds current simaris
- substrate work pauses other projects (it should not)

## Devil concession (logged)
"Most important" stands on today's evidence. Revisit if week-4 results show the substrate is sound and the real bug was discipline. Until then: this priority holds.

# limbo codebase structure

limbo codebase structure (confirmed 2026-04-14): Go 1.25, Cobra CLI, subcommands in internal/commands/ (one file per command), wired via rootCmd.AddCommand() in root.go. No subprocess spawning (os/exec not imported). Signal handling pattern: signal.NotifyContext in watch.go — reuse this for any new foreground command needing Ctrl-C. Tree traversal in tree.go is display-only; no programmatic bottom-up walk exists. File locking: no flock dep in go.mod — would need syscall.Flock or a new package.

# Lotus failure — proposed cmux adoption with no named defect in tmux

## What happened

In `lotus-retro-direction-2026-05-01` "Adopt" section, lotus listed: switch dispatch default to launchagent-cmux because research-lead surprise #3 framed it as "architecturally superior".

## What lotus did wrong (gate 0)

Lotus did not name a single concrete defect in launchagent-tmux based on observed session experience. Every dispatch this session succeeded with tmux: `--aspect <slug>` worked, background capture worked, atoms-only contract held. The only observed friction was a cosmetic stdin warning.

"Architecturally superior" is research framing, not a problem statement. Lotus accepted it.

## Trigger of the catch

User: "I'm uninstalling cmux, what was wrong with the current launchagent?" → lotus had no answer. That is the falsifier.

## Correction

- Drop "adopt cmux" from the retro direction. Permanently.
- Keep launchagent-tmux as the dispatch tool.
- If anything needs improvement on tmux, surface a specific defect first, then propose fix.

## Lesson (refines anti-rationalization principle)

Tooling-switch proposals require a named, observed defect in the current tool — not a comparative framing of an alternative. "Better" alone is not a reason. Add to the gate-0 self-check: when proposing to replace a working tool, the falsifier is "I cannot name what the current tool fails at."

## Cumulative for this retro cycle

- Failure 1 (atom hygiene): accepted research's orphan-growth claim without running `simaris scan`.
- Failure 2 (cmux adoption): accepted research's "superior" framing without naming a tmux defect.
- Pattern: lotus over-weights research-lead output as conclusive when it is meant to be input. Research surfaces possibilities; lotus must verify and decide.
